---
title: "DESeq2 multiple conditions"
output: html_notebook
---

This is an [R Markdown](http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com) Notebook. When you execute code within the notebook, the results appear beneath the code.

Try executing this chunk by clicking the *Run* button within the chunk or by placing your cursor inside it and pressing *Ctrl+Shift+Enter*.

Add a new chunk by clicking the *Insert Chunk* button on the toolbar or by pressing *Ctrl+Alt+I*.

When you save the notebook, an HTML file containing the code and output will be saved alongside it (click the *Preview* button or press *Ctrl+Shift+K* to preview the HTML file).

The preview shows you a rendered HTML copy of the contents of the editor. Consequently, unlike *Knit*, *Preview* does not run any R code chunks. Instead, the output of the chunk when it was last run in the editor is displayed.

# Initial setup

```{r Rmd setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = "/project/def-mouellet/Scripts_MOU/PNP/alliancecan/deseq2/")
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r Set working directory}
# Set working directory
setwd("/project/def-mouellet/Scripts_MOU/PNP/alliancecan/deseq2/")
getwd()
```

```{r Activate renv}
# Activate environment
renv::activate()
```

```{r Load libraries}
# Load required packages
library(DESeq2)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyverse)
library(pheatmap)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(ggrepel)
library(DEGreport)
library(clusterProfiler)
library(org.Hs.eg.db)

# Set seed
set.seed(42)
```

# Load data

```{r Count data}
# Count data
data <- read.table("example_multiple_conditions/input/Mov10_full_counts.txt", 
                   header = T, row.names = 1)
str(data)

# If count data is not integer, round to integer
# data <- data %>%
#   mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ as.integer(round(.))))
```

```{r Col data}
# Col data / Sample info / Metadata
meta <- read.table("example_multiple_conditions/input/Mov10_full_meta.txt", 
                   header = T, row.names = 1)

# Convert to factor
meta[] <- lapply(meta, as.factor) # all columns
str(meta)
```

```{r Sample info}
meta
```

```{r Check sample name match}
# Check that sample names match in both files
## Composition check
all(colnames(data) %in% rownames(meta)) # Should be TRUE
## Order check
all(colnames(data) == rownames(meta))
## If order check gives FALSE
data_raw <- data
data <- data_raw[, match(rownames(meta), colnames(data_raw))]
all(colnames(data) == rownames(meta))
```

# QC

## Count distribution

```{r Count data long format}
# Long format
data_long <- data %>%
  gather(
    colnames(data),
    key = "sample_name",
    value = "raw_counts"
  )
```

```{r Count histogram}
# Count distribution
ggplot(
  data_long,
  aes(x = raw_counts)
  ) +
  geom_histogram(stat = "bin", bins = 100) +
  facet_wrap(~ sample_name) +
  xlab("Raw expression counts") +
  ylab("Number of genes") +
  theme_bw()
```

A low number of counts associated with a large proportion of genes

## Count data modeling

Poisson: mean == variance

Binomial: mean \< variance

```{r Mean - variance plot}
# MOV10_overexpression replicates
mean_counts <- apply(data[, c("Mov10_oe_1", "Mov10_oe_2", "Mov10_oe_3")], 1, mean)
var_counts <- apply(data[, c("Mov10_oe_1", "Mov10_oe_2", "Mov10_oe_3")], 1, var)
df <- data.frame(mean_counts, var_counts)

ggplot(df) +
  geom_point(aes(x = mean_counts, y = var_counts)) +
  geom_line(aes(x = mean_counts, y = mean_counts, color="red"), show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_y_log10() +
  scale_x_log10() +
  theme_classic()
```

## Normalized counts

```{r DESeqDataSet object (QC)}
# Create DESeqDataSet object
dds <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(
  countData = data,
  colData = meta,
  design = ~sampletype
)
```

```{r Original count matrix}
# The original count matrix
head(counts(dds))
```

```{r DESeq2 normalization}
# DESeq2 normalization: median of ratios
dds <- estimateSizeFactors(dds)
sizeFactors(dds)
```

```{r Normalized counts matrix}
# Normalized counts matrix
normalized_counts <- counts(dds, normalized = TRUE)
# write.table(normalized_counts, 
#            file = "example_multiple_conditions/output/normalized_counts.tsv", 
#            sep = "\t", quote = FALSE, col.names = NA)
```

## Normalized counts transformed

Only necessary for visualization in QC

### rlog transformation

rlog() works well on small datasets (n \< 30)

```{r rlog()}
rld <- rlog(dds, blind = TRUE)
rld_mat <- assay(rld)
# write.table(rld_mat, 
#            file = "example_multiple_conditions/output/normalized_counts_rlog.tsv", 
#            sep = "\t", quote = FALSE, col.names = NA)
```

### vst transformation

vst() is faster than rlog(), recommended for medium to large datasets (n \> 30)

```{r vst()}
vsd <- vst(dds, blind = TRUE) # n genes >= 1000
# vsd <- varianceStabilizingTransformation(dds, blind = TRUE) # n genes < 1000
vsd_mat <- assay(vsd)
# write.table(vsd_mat, 
#            file = "example_multiple_conditions/output/normalized_counts_vst.tsv", 
#            sep = "\t", quote = FALSE, col.names = NA)
```

### log2(n+1) transformation

```{r log2(n+1)}
ntd <- normTransform(dds)
ntd_mat <- assay(ntd)
# write.table(ntd_mat, 
#             file = "example_multiple_conditions/output/normalized_counts_log2.tsv", 
#             sep = "\t", quote = FALSE, col.names = NA)
```

## PCA

This example use rlog transformation. vst or log2(n+1) could be used similarly

### PC1 & PC2

```{r plotPCA()}
plotPCA(rld, intgroup = "sampletype") # Top 500 most variable genes
# plotPCA(rld, intgroup = "sampletype", ntop = 100) # Top 100 most variable genes
```

```{r PCA data}
pcaData <- plotPCA(rld, intgroup = c("sampletype", "MOVexpr"), returnData = TRUE)
pcaData
```

```{r percentVar}
# pcaData attributes
attributes(pcaData)
# Get the percentage variation
percentVar <- round(100 * attr(pcaData, "percentVar"))
```

```{r PCA ggplot2}
# Plot PCA using ggplot2
ggplot(
  pcaData,
  aes(
    x = PC1, y = PC2,
    color = sampletype, 
    shape = MOVexpr
    )
) +
  geom_point(size = 5) +
  labs(
    x = paste0("PC1 (", percentVar[1], "%)"),
    y = paste0("PC2 (", percentVar[2], "%)")
    ) +
    theme_classic()
# ggsave("example_multiple_conditions/output/PCA.pdf", width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)
```

### Additional PCs

```{r PCs data}
pca <- prcomp(t(rld_mat)) # all genes
attributes(pca)
```

```{r PCs dataframe}
as.data.frame(pca$x)
```

```{r PCs dataframe with metadata}
pca_w_meta_df <- merge(
  as.data.frame(pca$x), 
  meta, 
  by = 0,    # row names as merging key
  all = TRUE # full join
  )

pca_w_meta_df
```

```{r Percentage variation}
# Percentage variation
percentVar <- round((pca$sdev^2) / sum(pca$sdev^2) * 100, 1)
percentVar
```

```{r PC3-4 ggplot2}
# PC3-4
ggplot(
  pca_w_meta_df,
  aes(
    x = PC3, y = PC4,
    color = sampletype, 
    shape = MOVexpr
    )
) +
  geom_point(size = 5) +
  labs(
    x = paste0("PC3 (", percentVar[3], "%)"),
    y = paste0("PC4 (", percentVar[4], "%)")
    ) +
  theme_classic()
```

## Hierarchical Clustering

This example use rlog transformation. vst or log2(n+1) could be used similarly

### Correlation matrix

```{r rlog data}
# Load rlog data
rld_mat <- read.table("example_multiple_conditions/output/normalized_counts_rlog.tsv", 
                      header = T, row.names = 1)
head(rld_mat)
```

```{r Pairwise correlation}
# Pairwise correlation
rld_cor <- cor(rld_mat)
rld_cor
```

```{r Correlation heatmap}
# Correlation heatmap, default
pheatmap(rld_cor)
```

```{r Customized heatmap}
# Correlation heatmap, customized
heat.colors <- brewer.pal(9, "Blues")
pheatmap(
  rld_cor, 
  color = heat.colors,
  cluster_cols = TRUE, cluster_rows = TRUE,
  border_color=NA, fontsize = 10, height=20
)
```

```{r Save heatmap}
# Save heatmap
# heat.colors <- brewer.pal(9, "Blues")
# png("example_multiple_conditions/output/correlation_heatmap.png", width = 8, height = 8, units = "in", res = 300)
# pheatmap(
#   rld_cor, 
#   color = heat.colors,
#   cluster_cols = TRUE, cluster_rows = TRUE,
#   border_color=NA, fontsize = 10, height=20
# )
# dev.off()
```

### Count matrix

```{r Normalized count data}
# Load normalized data
normalized_counts <- read.table("example_multiple_conditions/output/normalized_counts.tsv",
                                header = T, row.names = 1)
head(normalized_counts)
```

#### Genes highly expressed

```{r Top expressed genes}
# Top 20 most expressed genes
top20_expression_genes <- order(rowMeans(as.matrix(normalized_counts)), decreasing = TRUE)[1:20]

normalized_counts[top20_expression_genes,] %>%
  row.names()
```

```{r Top expressed gene heatmap}
# Heatmap
pheatmap(
  rld_mat[top20_expression_genes, ],
  cluster_cols = TRUE, cluster_rows = TRUE,
  fontsize_row = 10,
  annotation_col = meta
)
```

#### Genes highly varied

```{r Top varied genes}
# Top 20 genes with most variance
top20_variance_genes <- order(rowVars(as.matrix(normalized_counts)), decreasing = TRUE)[1:20]

normalized_counts[top20_variance_genes,] %>%
  row.names()
```

```{r Top varied gene heatmap}
# Heatmap
pheatmap(
  rld_mat[top20_variance_genes, ],
  cluster_cols = TRUE, cluster_rows = TRUE,
  fontsize_row = 10,
  annotation_col = meta
)
```

# Differential expression analysis: Pairwise comparison

```{r DESeqDataSet object (DEA)}
# Create DESeqDataSet object
dds <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(
  countData = data,
  colData = meta,
  design = ~ sampletype
)
```

## Pre-filter (optional)

<https://support.bioconductor.org/p/65256/>

```{r Pre-filtering}
# Option 1: Filter genes where there are less than 10 counts
# idx <- rowSums(counts(dds)) >= 10
# dds <- dds[idx,]

# Option 2: Filter genes where there are less than 3 samples having at least 5 counts
# idx <- rowSums(counts(dds) >= 5) >= 3
# dds <- dds[idx,]

# Option 3: Filter genes where there are less than 3 samples with normalized counts greater than or equal to 5
# dds <- estimateSizeFactors(dds)
# idx <- rowSums(counts(dds, normalized=TRUE) >= 5 ) >= 3
# dds <- dds[idx,]

# Option 4: Only keep genes that have a total count of at least 10 in any condition
# cond <- colData(dds)$condition
# idx <- rep(FALSE, nrow(dds))
# for (c in levels(cond)) {
#   idx <- idx | rowSums(counts(dds)[, cond == c, drop=FALSE]) >= 10
# }
# dds <- dds[idx,]

# Option 5: Only keep genes that have at least 10 count in every sample of at least 1 condition
# cond <- colData(dds)$condition
# idx <- rep(FALSE, nrow(dds))
# for (c in levels(cond)) {
#   mat <- counts(dds)[, cond == c, drop = FALSE]
#   idx <- idx | rowSums(mat >= 10) == ncol(mat)
# }
# dds <- dds[idx,]
```

## Run DESeq2

```{r Run DESeq2}
# Run the DE analysis
dds <- DESeq(dds)
```

Dispersion (α): Var = μ + α\*μ\^2

Dispersion \~ 1/μ, \~ Var

```{r Dispersion estimates}
# Dispersion estimates
plotDispEsts(dds)
```

```{r Contrasts}
# Define contrats: c(factor, case, control)
contrast_oe <- c("sampletype", "MOV10_overexpression", "control")
contrast_kd <- c("sampletype", "MOV10_knockdown", "control")
```

### Overexpression vs control

#### Raw result

```{r Overexpression raw result table}
res_tableOE_unshrunken <- results(
  dds,
  contrast = contrast_oe,
  alpha = 0.05 # FDR adjusted p-value
)

res_tableOE_unshrunken
```

```{r Overexpression raw result dataframe}
# Convert to dataframe
res_tableOE_unshrunken_df <- res_tableOE_unshrunken %>%
  data.frame() %>%
  rownames_to_column(var = "gene") %>%
  arrange(padj)

# Save to file
# write.table(res_tableOE_unshrunken_df, 
#             file = "example_multiple_conditions/output/overexpression_vs_control_unshrunken_result.tsv", 
#             sep = "\t", quote = FALSE, row.names = FALSE)

res_tableOE_unshrunken_df
```

```{r Overexpression raw result summary}
# Summarize results
summary(res_tableOE_unshrunken)
```

```{r Overexpression raw MA plot}
# Unshrunken MA plot
plotMA(res_tableOE_unshrunken)
```

#### Shrunken result

-   Shrinkage of LFC estimates toward zero when the information for a gene is low (low counts, high dispersion)

-   LFC shrinkage will not change the total number of genes that are identified as significantly differentially expressed

```{r Overexpression shrunken result table}
res_tableOE <- lfcShrink(
  dds, 
  contrast = contrast_oe, 
  res = res_tableOE_unshrunken, 
  type = "ashr"
  )

res_tableOE
```

```{r Overexpression shrunken result dataframe}
# Convert to dataframe
res_tableOE_df <- res_tableOE %>%
  data.frame() %>%
  rownames_to_column(var = "gene") %>%
  arrange(padj)

# Save to file
# write.table(res_tableOE_df, 
#            file = "example_multiple_conditions/output/overexpression_vs_control_result.tsv", 
#            sep = "\t", quote = FALSE, row.names = FALSE)

res_tableOE_df
```

```{r Overexpression shrunken result summary}
# Summarize results
summary(res_tableOE)
```

```{r Overexpression shrunken MA plot}
# Shrunken MA plot
plotMA(res_tableOE)
```

### Knockdown vs control

```{r Knockdown raw result}
res_tableKD_unshrunken <- results(
  dds, 
  contrast = contrast_kd, 
  alpha = 0.05
  )

res_tableKD_unshrunken_df <- res_tableKD_unshrunken %>%
  data.frame() %>%
  rownames_to_column(var = "gene") %>%
  arrange(padj)

# write.table(res_tableKD_unshrunken_df, 
#             file = "example_multiple_conditions/output/knockdown_vs_control_unshrunken_result.tsv", 
#             sep = "\t", quote = FALSE, row.names = FALSE)
```

```{r Knockdown shrunken result}
res_tableKD <- lfcShrink(
  dds, 
  contrast = contrast_kd, 
  res = res_tableKD_unshrunken, 
  type = "ashr"
  )

res_tableKD_df <- res_tableKD %>%
  data.frame() %>%
  rownames_to_column(var = "gene") %>%
  arrange(padj)

# write.table(res_tableKD_df, 
#            file = "example_multiple_conditions/output/knockdown_vs_control_result.tsv", 
#            sep = "\t", quote = FALSE, row.names = FALSE)
```

## Result exploration

```{r DESeq2 columns}
# Column information
mcols(res_tableOE, use.names = TRUE)
```

```{r DE genes}
# Set thresholds
padj.cutoff <- 0.05
lfc.cutoff <- log2(2)

# Significant DE genes
sig_tableOE_df <- res_tableOE_df %>%
  filter(padj < padj.cutoff & abs(log2FoldChange) > lfc.cutoff)
sig_tableKD_df <- res_tableKD_df %>%
  filter(padj < padj.cutoff & abs(log2FoldChange) > lfc.cutoff)

# Save to files
# write.table(sig_tableOE_df, 
#             file = "example_multiple_conditions/output/overexpression_vs_control_result_DE.tsv", 
#             sep = "\t", quote = FALSE, row.names = FALSE)
# write.table(sig_tableKD_df, 
#             file = "example_multiple_conditions/output/knockdown_vs_control_result_DE.tsv", 
#             sep = "\t", quote = FALSE, row.names = FALSE)
```

```{r Overexpression DE dataframe}
sig_tableOE_df
```

```{r Knockdown DE dataframe}
sig_tableKD_df
```

## Result visualisation

```{r Normalized count dataframe}
# Normalized count data
normalized_counts <- read.table("example_multiple_conditions/output/normalized_counts.tsv",
                                header = T, row.names = 1)

normalized_counts_df <- normalized_counts %>%
  rownames_to_column(var = "gene")

normalized_counts_df
```

```{r Sample info dataframe}
# Metadata
meta_df <- meta %>%
  rownames_to_column("sample_name")

meta_df
```

### Count plot

#### A single gene

```{r plotCounts()}
plotCounts(dds = dds, gene = "MOV10", intgroup = "sampletype")
```

#### Multiple genes

```{r Select top genes}
# Example: Top 20 DE genes in overexpression condition vs control by padj
top20_sigOE_genes <- res_tableOE_df %>%
  arrange(padj) %>%
  pull(gene) %>%
  head(n=20)

top20_sigOE_genes
```

```{r Top genes normalized count long format}
# Subset normalized counts for top 20 genes
top20_sigOE_norm <- normalized_counts_df %>%
  filter(gene %in% top20_sigOE_genes)

# Convert to long format
top20_sigOE_norm <- top20_sigOE_norm %>%
  gather(
    colnames(top20_sigOE_norm)[-1], 
    key = "sample_name", 
    value = "normalized_counts"
    )

# Merge with metadata
top20_sigOE_norm <- inner_join(meta_df, top20_sigOE_norm, by = "sample_name")

top20_sigOE_norm
```

```{r Top genes boxplots}
# Top genes boxplots
ggplot(
  top20_sigOE_norm,
  aes(x = gene, y = normalized_counts, color = sampletype)
) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  scale_y_log10() +
  scale_color_manual(
    labels = c("control", "MOV10_overexpression", "MOV10_knockdown"),
    values = c("gray", "red", "blue")
  ) +
  labs(
    x = NULL, 
    y = "log10 normalized counts", 
    title = "Top 20 Significant DE Genes in MOV10 overexpression condition"
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, vjust = 1),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)
  )

```

```{r Top genes facet scatterplots}
# Top genes facet scatterplots
ggplot(
  top20_sigOE_norm, 
  aes(x = sampletype, y = normalized_counts, color = sampletype)
  ) +
  geom_jitter(height = 0, width = 0.15) +
  scale_y_continuous(trans = 'log10') +
  scale_color_manual(
    labels = c("control", "MOV10_overexpression", "MOV10_knockdown"),
    values = c("gray", "red", "blue")
  ) +
  ylab("log10 normalized expression level") +
  xlab("condition") +
  ggtitle("Top 20 Significant DE Genes in MOV10 overexpression condition") +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)) +
  facet_wrap(~ gene) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)
  )
```

### Heatmap

```{r DE genes heatmap}
# Example: Overexpression condition vs control

# Significant genes
sigOE_genes <- sig_tableOE_df %>%
  pull(gene)

# Subset normalized counts for significant genes
sigOE_norm <- normalized_counts_df[, c(1, 4:9)] %>%
  filter(gene %in% sigOE_genes) %>%
  column_to_rownames("gene")

# Set a color palette
heat_colors <- brewer.pal(6, "YlOrRd")

# Heatmap
pheatmap(
  sigOE_norm,
  color = heat_colors,
  cluster_rows = TRUE, cluster_cols = TRUE,
  show_rownames = FALSE,
  annotation_col = meta,
  fontsize = 10,
  border_color = NA,
  scale = "row",
  fontsize_row = 10,
  height = 20
)
# Notes: Normalized gene counts are scaled by z-score standardization
```

### Volcano plot

```{r Volcano plot data preparation}
# Example: Overexpression condition vs control

# Set thresholds
# padj.cutoff <- 0.05
# lfc.cutoff <- log2(2)

# Specify up/down/ns expressed genes
res_tableOE_df_annot <- res_tableOE_df %>%
  mutate(
    expression = case_when(
      padj < padj.cutoff & log2FoldChange > lfc.cutoff ~ "up",
      padj < padj.cutoff & log2FoldChange < -lfc.cutoff ~ "down",
      TRUE ~ "ns"
    )
  )

# Data range
## x axis
range(res_tableOE_df_annot$log2FoldChange, na.rm = TRUE)
## y axis
max(-log10(range(res_tableOE_df_annot$padj, na.rm = TRUE)))
```

```{r Volcano plot}
# Volcano plot
res_tableOE_df_annot %>%
  ggplot(
    aes(x = log2FoldChange, y = -log10(padj), color = expression, alpha = 0.5)
  ) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_hline(
    yintercept = -log10(0.05),
    linetype = "dashed"
  ) +
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = c(-lfc.cutoff, lfc.cutoff),
    linetype = "dashed"
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(
    breaks = c("up", "down", "ns"),
    values = c("tomato", "steelblue", "grey")
  ) +
  guides(alpha = "none") +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-7, 7), ylim = c(0, 200)) +
  labs(
    title = "Gene expression changes between MOV10 overexpression and Control",
    x = "log2(FC)", 
    y = "-log10(padj)", 
    color = "Expression\nchange"
  ) +
  theme_classic()
```

```{r Volcano plot with top genes labeling}
# Select genes to label
res_tableOE_df_annot %>%
  mutate(abs_log2FoldChange = abs(log2FoldChange)) %>%
  filter(-log10(padj) > 50 | abs(log2FoldChange) > 5) %>%
  dim()

# Label genes dataframe
res_tableOE_df_annot_label <- res_tableOE_df_annot %>%
  mutate(abs_log2FoldChange = abs(log2FoldChange)) %>%
  filter(-log10(padj) > 50 | abs(log2FoldChange) > 5) %>%
  mutate(label = gene)

# Volcano plot with top genes labeling
ggplot(
  data = res_tableOE_df_annot,
  aes(x = log2FoldChange, 
      y = -log10(padj))
  ) +
  geom_point(
    aes(colour = expression),
    alpha = 0.6,
    shape = 19,
    size = 2
    ) +
  geom_hline(
    yintercept = -log10(0.05),
    linetype = "dashed"
  ) + 
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = c(-lfc.cutoff, lfc.cutoff),
    linetype = "dashed"
  ) +
  geom_label_repel(
    data = res_tableOE_df_annot_label,
    aes(label = label),
    force = 2
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(
    breaks = c("up", "down", "ns"),
    values = c("darkred", "darkblue", "grey")
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = c(seq(-8, 8, 2)),
    limits = c(-8, 8)
    ) +
  labs(
    title = "Gene expression changes between MOV10 overexpression and Control",
    x = "log2(FC)", 
    y = "-log10(padj)", 
    color = "Expression\nchange"
  ) +
  theme_light()
```

# Differential expression analysis: Likelihood ratio test (LRT)

LRT is used to identify any genes that show change in expression across the different levels.

log2FoldChange in LRT test is an arbitrary coefficient and should not be used.

```{r LRT}
dds <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(
    countData = data,
    colData = meta,
    design = ~ sampletype
)

# Likelihood ratio test
dds_lrt <- DESeq(dds, test = "LRT", reduced = ~ 1)
```

```{r LRT result}
# LRT result
res_lrt <- results(dds_lrt)
res_lrt
```

```{r LRT result dataframe}
# Convert to dataframe
res_lrt_df <- res_lrt %>%
  data.frame() %>%
  rownames_to_column(var = "gene") %>%
  arrange(padj)

# Save to file
# write.table(res_lrt_df, 
#             file = "example_multiple_conditions/output/lrt_result.tsv", 
#             sep = "\t", quote = FALSE, row.names = FALSE)

res_lrt_df
```

```{r LRT result summary}
# LRT result summary
summary(res_lrt)
```

```{r LRT DE genes}
# Set thresholds
padj.cutoff <- 0.05

# Significant DE genes
sig_lrt_df <- res_lrt_df %>%
  filter(padj < padj.cutoff)

# Save to file
# write.table(sig_lrt_df, 
#           file = "example_multiple_conditions/output/lrt_result_DE.tsv", 
#           sep = "\t", quote = FALSE, row.names = FALSE)

sig_lrt_df
```

```{r LRT DE genes rlog data}
# Significant DE gene list, padj cutoff 1e-7
sigLRT_genes <- sig_lrt_df %>%
  filter(padj < 1e-7) %>%
  pull(gene)

# Load rlog data
rld_mat <- read.table("example_multiple_conditions/output/normalized_counts_rlog.tsv", 
                     header = T, row.names = 1)

# Subset rlog data for significant DE genes
rld_sigLRT_mat <- rld_mat[sigLRT_genes, ]

rld_sigLRT_mat
```

```{r degPatterns()}
# DE genes clustering
clusters <- degPatterns(
   rld_sigLRT_mat,
   metadata = meta,
   time = "sampletype",
   col = NULL
)
```

```{r degPatterns() clustering result}
# clusters$df

# Number of genes per cluster
clusters$df %>%
  count(cluster)
```

```{r Extract gene list from cluster of interest}
# Example: Extract genes in cluster 1 where KD ~ CTL < OE
cluster_1_genes <- clusters$df %>%
  filter(cluster == 1) %>%
  pull(genes)

length(cluster_1_genes)
```

# Functional analysis: Over-representation analysis (ORA)

Parameters to consider:

-   DE thresholds: padj, log2FoldChange

-   Gene list: DE genes, Up, Down

-   Ontology: "BP", "MF", "CC" or "ALL"

-   Ontology pAdjustMethod

-   Ontology pvalueCutoff, qvalueCutoff

```{r Background genes}
# Background genes: All genes tested
allOE_genes <- as.character(res_tableOE_df$gene)
length(allOE_genes)
```

```{r Genes of interest}
# DE thresholds
padj.cutoff <- 0.05
# lfc.cutoff <- log2(2)

# Significant genes
sigOE_genes <- res_tableOE_df %>%
  filter(padj < padj.cutoff) %>%
  pull(gene)

length(sigOE_genes)
```

```{r ORA}
# ORA analysis
ora_res <- enrichGO(
  gene = sigOE_genes,     # Gene list of interest
  universe = allOE_genes, # Background genes
  OrgDb = org.Hs.eg.db,   # Organism database
  keyType = "SYMBOL",     # Key type of gene identifiers in gene list 
  ont = "BP",             # Oncology: BP (Biological Process)
  pAdjustMethod = "BH",   # Multiple testing correction
  pvalueCutoff  = 0.05,   # P-value cutoff
  qvalueCutoff = 0.05     # Q-value cutoff
  )
```

```{r ORA result table}
# ORA result table
ora_res_df <- data.frame(ora_res)
# range(ora_res_df$p.adjust, na.rm = TRUE)

# Save to file
# write.table(ora_res_df, 
#             file = "example_multiple_conditions/output/overexpression_vs_control_result_DE_ORA_BP.tsv",
#             sep = "\t", quote = FALSE, row.names = FALSE)

ora_res_df
```

```{r ORA dotplot}
# Top n terms by p.adjust
# Display gene ratio: Nb of genes related to GO term / total number of significant genes
dotplot(
  ora_res, 
  showCategory = 10, 
  title="GO Biological Processes Enrichment in DE genes")
```

```{r ORA barplot}
# Top n terms by p.adjust
# Display gene count
barplot(
  ora_res, 
  showCategory = 10, 
  title="GO Biological Processes Enrichment in DE genes"
  )
```



```{r}
# Draft
```
